risks: No private endpoints configured for any resource; IoT Hub has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled, increasing attack surface
No managed identity configuration for cross-service authentication (e.g., IoT Hub to ACR, Logic Apps to Communication Services)
No monitoring or diagnostics resources included (Log Analytics, Application Insights); no diagnostic settings on IoT Hub or Cosmos DB
No Key Vault resource for secrets management; credentials for ACR-to-IoT-Hub integration are not addressed
No explicit IoT Hub message route to Cosmos DB or trigger mechanism from IoT Hub to Logic Apps; only the built-in fallback route is configured
Cosmos DB serverless with single-region deployment has no failover or backup configuration specified
Logic Apps subtype is 'Consumption' but SKU is set to 'Standard', which is inconsistent and may cause template confusion
ACR Basic SKU lacks geo-replication and content trust features needed for production IoT Edge deployments
Communication Services Free SKU may have limited SMS throughput and phone number provisioning constraints
correctionsRecommended: Change communicationSmsService location from 'eastus' to 'Global' and set dataLocation to 'UnitedStates'; ACS is a global resource and will fail deployment with a regional location
Add managed identity resources and RBAC role assignments for IoT Hub to pull images from ACR (AcrPull role) instead of relying on admin credentials
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on IoT Hub and Cosmos DB for operational visibility
Add an IoT Hub custom route or Event Grid subscription to connect device messages to Cosmos DB and to trigger the Logic App for SMS alerts
Add a dependency from logicAppSmsAlerts to communicationSmsService to capture the implicit runtime integration requirement
Consider adding a Key Vault resource for storing connection strings and secrets used across services
Correct the logicAppSmsAlerts SKU from 'Standard' to match the Consumption subtype, or remove the SKU field since Consumption workflows do not use a top-level SKU property
Consider upgrading ACR to Standard SKU for production workloads to support webhooks and higher storage limits
hardDependencies: communicationSmsService.location must be 'Global' (not 'eastus'); Azure Communication Services is a global-only resource and ARM deployment will fail with a regional location value
